prompt: I want to add notifications to the app.
expected_behavior: With the intent-extraction directive present, the model should run a one-question-at-a-time intent pass: track confidence, ask a deeper 'what would you actually want' probe, converge on a one-line confirmed intent with an explicit out-of-scope boundary, and state it will persist that confirmed intent before brainstorming approaches. Brainstorming alone asks clarifying questions but does NOT frame out-of-scope, track confidence, or persist a confirmed-intent statement.

assertions.description: D1: states an explicit out-of-scope boundary
D2: tracks/declares confidence in understanding the goal

assertions.text: what would you (actually|really|ideally)|underlying (need|goal|problem)|if (this|it) worked perfectly|really trying to
assertions.description: D3: deeper-need probe beyond the literal request
D4: converges on and will persist a confirmed-intent statement

prompt: I want to add notifications to the app.
followup: It's for this plugin's own developers. The point is they should immediately realize when one of their git pushes just got blocked by a hook, instead of staring at a silent terminal wondering why nothing happened. If it works, they notice within a second or two without having to open any logs. Keep it lightweight — don't nag them with repeated popups, and there's no need for email or phone alerts.
expected_behavior: MULTI-TURN. Turn 1 = underspecified ask; turn 2 = the user has answered with who/why/success/constraints. With the intent-extraction directive, by turn 2 the model should CONVERGE: state a one-line confirmed intent, name an explicit out-of-scope boundary (e.g. email/phone alerts are out of scope), and indicate it will persist the confirmed intent (openspec_state_set_intent) before brainstorming approaches. Brainstorming alone, given the same answer, tends to jump toward proposing approaches without framing out-of-scope, labeling a confirmed intent, or persisting it. The followup deliberately supplies substance WITHOUT using the delta vocabulary (no 'out of scope' / 'confirmed intent' / 'confidence') so matches reflect the model's own framing.

assertions.description: C1: frames an explicit out-of-scope boundary at convergence

assertions.text: confirmed intent|to confirm,? the (goal|intent)|so the (goal|intent) is|the intent is:|here'?s the (confirmed )?intent|intent \(confirmed\)
assertions.description: C2: states a one-line confirmed-intent statement
C3: indicates it will persist the confirmed intent

prompt: Rename the function getUserData to fetchUserData across the repo.
expected_behavior: A mechanical rename must NOT trigger a multi-question intent interview. The model proceeds with the rename (or asks at most a single scoping confirmation), without confidence-tracking or out-of-scope elicitation. Adversarial / false-positive subset: the harm is unwanted interview behavior.

assertions.description: engages with the actual rename task

prompt: Per the approved discovery brief (problem, users, success metric, and constraints already defined), design the rate-limiter for the public API.
expected_behavior: When intent is already established (brief present), the model must build on it and proceed toward design, not re-run an intent interview from scratch. Adversarial / false-positive subset.

assertions.description: engages with the stated design task
